_field_notes.fit: Curated suitability of the target for this source component.
_field_notes.latency_class: oltp_low (<10ms) | oltp_moderate | analytical (sec+) | messaging — factual service property.

vsam_ksds.description: Keyed record file with single-key access patterns

DynamoDB.when_to_choose: Single-key or key+sort access; sub-10ms latency; scale beyond one-region write ceiling
DynamoDB.caveats: Secondary indexes cost extra; no JOINs; requires access-pattern redesign
DynamoDB.fsi_notes: Point-in-time recovery, KMS-at-rest, Streams for audit are standard. Good SOX/FFIEC posture.

Aurora_PostgreSQL.when_to_choose: Need ad-hoc queries, JOINs, or want to consolidate VSAM+DB2
Aurora_PostgreSQL.caveats: Higher baseline latency than DynamoDB; vertical-scale ceiling
Aurora_PostgreSQL.fsi_notes: Familiar relational control model; easier to map SOX change-control.

S3_plus_Athena.when_to_choose: Archival / warm analytical access; low write volume
S3_plus_Athena.fsi_notes: Object Lock + Versioning give strong retention evidence.

Keyspaces.when_to_choose: Only if team already has Cassandra skills
Keyspaces.fsi_notes: Acceptable but less common in FSI.

S3.when_to_choose: Default choice; flat-file semantics map cleanly
S3.caveats: Design object-key schema carefully to avoid hot partitions
S3.fsi_notes: Object Lock + CloudTrail data events give strong audit trail.

EFS.when_to_choose: Only if lift-and-shift with POSIX filesystem assumption
EFS.caveats: Expensive at scale; rarely the right long-term answer
EFS.fsi_notes: Possible but unusual in FSI migrations.

Aurora_PostgreSQL.when_to_choose: Default; best tooling via AWS SCT + DMS
Aurora_PostgreSQL.caveats: Stored procedures and triggers need translation (PL/pgSQL); some DB2 numeric types need mapping
Aurora_PostgreSQL.fsi_notes: Mature FSI footprint; supports IAM-based DB auth, KMS, CloudTrail, enhanced monitoring.

Aurora_MySQL.caveats: Less feature parity with DB2 than Postgres (e.g., window functions, types)
Aurora_MySQL.fsi_notes: Acceptable; fewer FSI reference patterns than Postgres.

RDS_Oracle.when_to_choose: Customer already has Oracle licensing and wants minimal re-work
RDS_Oracle.fsi_notes: Familiar control surface; often used as a staging step.

Redshift.when_to_choose: Only for analytics offload, not OLTP
Redshift.fsi_notes: Use alongside Aurora for regulatory reporting workloads.

DynamoDB.when_to_choose: If willing to remodel hierarchy as document/key-value
DynamoDB.caveats: Major data-model rework; joins have to move to application

Aurora_PostgreSQL_with_JSONB.when_to_choose: If hierarchy maps naturally to nested JSON
Aurora_PostgreSQL_with_JSONB.caveats: Performance can be acceptable but requires careful index design
Aurora_PostgreSQL_with_JSONB.fsi_notes: Easier to map existing SOX controls.

Neptune.when_to_choose: If relationships are graph-like, not tree-like

Rocket_Enterprise_Server_on_EC2.when_to_choose: Rehost/replatform — preserve transaction semantics. Featured AWS replatform partner per 2025-12-12 blog.
Rocket_Enterprise_Server_on_EC2.caveats: Carries monolith shape forward. AWS M2 Managed Runtime (Micro Focus/Rocket flavor) closed to new customers 2025-11-07 — must self-manage on EC2 or use partner-managed runtime.
Rocket_Enterprise_Server_on_EC2.fsi_notes: Rocket has deep FSI relationships from the acquired Micro Focus book. Familiar audit evidence pattern.

AWS_Transform_for_mainframe_output.when_to_choose: Automated Refactor — deterministic COBOL/CICS → Java (Spring Boot) on ECS/Fargate via AWS Transform. GA 2025-05-15. Blu Age technology under the hood.
AWS_Transform_for_mainframe_output.caveats: Regression surface larger than rehost; Functional Equivalence Testing required. Human-in-the-Loop validation gate required per AWS guidance.
AWS_Transform_for_mainframe_output.fsi_notes: Inherits AWS-native controls (IAM, KMS, CloudTrail) post-refactor. Aligned to DORA operational-resilience evidence.

AWS_Transform_plus_Claude_Code_reimagine.when_to_choose: Reimagine — reverse-engineer CICS transactions into business rules with AWS Transform, then forward-engineer as microservices with Claude Code. Best when the target must be event-driven / API-first, not just cloud-hosted. See 2026-05-08 blog.
AWS_Transform_plus_Claude_Code_reimagine.caveats: 18-36+ months. Requires business appetite for architecture change. Domain-decomposition errors compound; SME review of AI-generated specs is mandatory.
AWS_Transform_plus_Claude_Code_reimagine.fsi_notes: Best-in-class transformation opportunity. Structured business rules (Given/When/Then) outlive retiring SMEs — genuine institutional-knowledge preservation.

AWS_M2_MicroFocus_existing_only.when_to_choose: ONLY for existing AWS M2 Managed Runtime customers. Closed to new customers 2025-11-07.
AWS_M2_MicroFocus_existing_only.caveats: Not available for new engagements. For new-customer rehost, use Rocket_Enterprise_Server_on_EC2.
AWS_M2_MicroFocus_existing_only.fsi_notes: Existing FSI customers can continue; new ones must plan for self-managed or partner-managed.

ECS_Fargate_custom_rewrite.when_to_choose: Greenfield rewrite without AWS Transform — highest ceiling, highest cost. Generally superseded by AWS_Transform_plus_Claude_Code_reimagine for structured outcomes.
ECS_Fargate_custom_rewrite.caveats: 18-36+ months; only justified when AWS Transform doesn't fit the source characteristics
ECS_Fargate_custom_rewrite.fsi_notes: Full control of modern stack; biggest transformation opportunity.

API_Gateway_plus_Lambda.when_to_choose: For lightweight, stateless transactions after decomposition (post-reimagine target-side pattern)
API_Gateway_plus_Lambda.caveats: Cold-start sensitivity for strict latency SLAs
API_Gateway_plus_Lambda.fsi_notes: Good for customer-facing API front door.

jcl_batch.description: Batch job scheduling and dependency management

AWS_Batch_plus_EventBridge_Scheduler.caveats: Dependency DAGs must be re-expressed (Step Functions or scheduler dependencies)
AWS_Batch_plus_EventBridge_Scheduler.fsi_notes: CloudTrail + Step Functions history give strong audit evidence.

MWAA_Airflow.caveats: Ops overhead; team must learn Airflow

Control_M_on_EC2.when_to_choose: Customer already uses Control-M; keeps scheduler parity

Amazon_MQ_RabbitMQ.when_to_choose: Need AMQP semantics similar to MQ
Amazon_MQ_RabbitMQ.caveats: Different operational model than classic MQ

MSK_Kafka.caveats: Not a drop-in replacement; design rework required
MSK_Kafka.fsi_notes: Strong at high volume; requires explicit retention policies for compliance.

SQS_plus_SNS.when_to_choose: Point-to-point and fan-out with simple semantics
